description: Study 6: Study 5 scaled to n=24/condition. 4 problems x 2 variants x 3 forced conditions x 6 runs = 144 cells, pre-registered (H6: modality equivalence is not a small-n artifact).
motivation: Confirm the Study-5 modality-equivalence result at confidence-interval scale before building the headline claim on it.
formal_results: Catch rates: forced_textual 24/24, forced_visual 23/24, forced_reflection 23/24; pairwise Fisher's exact p = 1.0000. All conditions also got every true variant correct.
results_description: Modality equivalence holds at scale: no detectable difference between visual, numeric, and reflection verification.
conclusions: H6 supported: the modality-equivalence result is robust, not a small-n artifact.
